column1: # All species use map-based distribution with life-stage and seasonal maps
# Cod: Steps 4-15 = Mar-Aug. VERIFIED — Bleil et al. (2009, doi:10.1111/j.1439-0426.2008.01172.x):
#   Bornholm Basin main spawning May-Aug, with shift to peak Jul since 1990s.
#   Western Baltic spring spawning Mar-Apr. Combined Mar-Aug covers both stocks.
# Herring: Steps 4-11 = Mar-Jun. VERIFIED — Western Baltic spring-spawning (WBSS)
#   herring spawns Mar-Jun (Ory et al. 2024, doi:10.1111/jfb.15811). Central Baltic
#   spring-spawning also peaks Apr-Jun. Autumn-spawning herring NOT represented.
# Sprat: Steps 6-13 = Apr-Jul. VERIFIED — Haslob et al. (2013, doi:10.1016/j.fishres.2012.08.002):
#   Baltic sprat spawning Jan-Jun, peak Apr-Jun in Bornholm Basin. Config covers peak.
# Flounder: Steps 2-9 = Feb-May. VERIFIED — pelagic spawner (P. flesus) spawns Feb-May

column2: demersal spawner (P. solemdali) spawns Apr-Jun coastal.

column1: #   Config captures the pelagic spawner timing well.
# Perch: Steps 8-11 = May-Jun. VERIFIED — standard Baltic coastal perch spawning.
# sp0: Cod — juvenile (age 0-1), adult (1-4), spawning adults (4+, Mar-Aug)
# sp1: Herring — juvenile (0-1), adult (1+), spring spawning (Mar-Jun, coastal),
#   autumn spawning (Sep-Nov, coastal). Both spawning components use coastal areas.
#   Autumn-spawning herring are ~20-30% of Baltic herring reproductive output
#   (mainly western Baltic SD22-24 and some central Baltic).
# sp2: Sprat — juvenile (0-1), adult (1+), spawning (Apr-Jul, deep)
# sp3: Flounder — juvenile (0-2, coastal), adult (2+), spawning (Feb-May, deep south)
# sp4: Perch — juvenile (0-2, coastal), adult (2+, coastal), spawning (May-Jun)
# sp5: Pike-perch — juvenile (0-2, coastal), adult (2+), spawning (Apr-Jun)
# sp6: Smelt — juvenile (0-1, everywhere), adult (1-6, pelagic), spawning (Feb-May, coastal)
# sp7: Stickleback — juvenile (0-1, everywhere), adult (1+, coastal), spawning (May-Jul)
